perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Receiving Party or anyone to whom it discloses the Confidential Information receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information under the terms of a subpoena or other order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by another governmental agency or as 3 otherwise required pursuant to law, regulation or other legal process, the Receiving Party shall (if legally practicable or permitted) (a) promptly notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (b) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking steps to resist or narrow such request, (c) if disclosure of such Confidential Information or Discussion Information is required, furnish only such portion of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information as the Receiving Party is advised by outside legal counsel is legally required to be disclosed and (d) take commercially reasonable efforts to cooperate with the Company (at the Company’s expense) in its efforts to obtain a protective order or other relief to prevent the disclosure of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to such portion of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information, as applicable, that is required to be disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Receiving Party receives a request to disclose Confidential Information under a subpoena or court order, the Receiving Party will follow standard legal practices.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific steps the Receiving Party must take when facing a subpoena (notify the company, consult, narrow the request, cooperate), and replacing them with 'standard legal practices,' the contract introduces ambiguity. It creates an in-text contradiction because it no longer clearly defines the Receiving Party's responsibilities, making enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Disclosure Pursuant to Law, Regulation, Subpoena or Court Order. In the event that the Receiving Party receives a request to disclose Confidential Information under a subpoena or court order, the Receiving Party will follow standard legal practices. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party or its Representatives will be permitted to disclose the Confidential Information or any portion thereof upon the routine request of any government or regulatory body having or claiming to have authority to regulate or oversee any aspect of your or your Representatives’ business of that of its affiliates, provided that they shall advise the governmental or regulatory body of the confidential nature of such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies precise actions the Receiving Party must undertake upon receiving a subpoena. The changed text replaces these specific actions with 'standard legal practices', introducing ambiguity. The contradicted text, located in the same place as the changed text, emphasizes 'standard legal practices' while also noting exceptions to this practice. This introduces ambiguity, as the standard legal practices are not defined, and the receiving party is given the ability to decide whether the standard legal practices are followed or the exceptions. Therefore, the modified text creates a contradiction, as the obligations of the receiving party are no longer clear.

perturbation.original_text: Without the Company’s prior written consent, which may be withheld by the Company in its sole discretion, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall direct its Representatives not to) initiate (other than through the Company’s financial and legal advisors, as designated by the Company in writing and other than contacts made or initiated in the ordinary course of business) any (a) communication concerning the Confidential Information, (b) requests for meetings with management of the Company in connection with the possible cooperation between the parties or (c) communication relating to the business of the Company or its affiliates or the possible cooperation, in each case, with any officer, director or employee of the Company or any of its affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall communicate about the possible cooperation only through designated channels.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific restrictions on communication (no direct contact with officers/employees), the modified text becomes vague. The term 'designated channels' lacks a clear definition within the document. Which creates an in-text contradiction, because it does not specifies who these desingated channels are.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Communications . The Receiving Party shall communicate about the possible cooperation only through designated channels. The foregoing shall not apply to communication with the Chief Executive Officer of the Company Bill Carey.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies restrictions on communication with the company (officers, directors, etc.) without prior written consent. The changed text, 'The Receiving Party shall communicate about the possible cooperation only through designated channels,' omits these specific restrictions and introduces the vague term 'designated channels.' The contradicted text reinforces this vagueness but adds an exception for the CEO (Bill Carey), showcasing that the designated channels does not apply to him. Since the term 'designated channels' is undefined, it is not clear who the receiving party can communicate with, outside of the CEO, creating a contradiction, because it doesn't align with the original text's detailed limitations. The omission creates ambiguity, and the changed text, in conjunction with the contradicted text, creates a direct conflict due to lack of clear guidance on communication, leading to confusion and potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect one (1) year from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate as determined by Company policy.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the explicit one-year termination clause and replacing it with 'Company policy' creates ambiguity. Company policy is not specified or accessible within the context of the agreement. It introduces an in-text contradiction because it provides no termination date or a duration creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 18. Termination . This Agreement shall terminate as determined by Company policy. [Remainder of Page Intentionally Left Blank]
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a definite termination date (one year). The changed text replaces this with 'as determined by Company policy,' creating ambiguity and uncertainty. The contradicted text, 'This Agreement shall terminate as determined by Company policy', does not resolve this uncertainty. The change directly contradicts the implicit expectation of a fixed term and renders the termination clause unenforceable due to lack of a specific timeframe or accessible policy. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, where essential language (the termination date) is removed, creating a contradiction with the implied expectation of a defined contract duration and directly conflicts by not specifying when it can terminate, making the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party and its Representatives: (a) irrevocably and unconditionally consent and submit to the jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in the State of New York for purposes of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement: (b) agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by U.S. registered mail to the address set forth at the end of this Agreement shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives; (c) irrevocably and unconditionally waive any objection to the laying of venue of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement in any state or federal court located in the State of New York; and (d) irrevocably and unconditionally waive the right to plead or claim, and irrevocably and unconditionally agree not to plead or claim, that any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement that is brought in any state or federal court located in the State of New York has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party and its Representatives agree to adhere to mutually agreeable dispute resolution methods.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific details about jurisdiction, venue, and service of process, and replacing it with 'mutually agreeable dispute resolution methods,' the modified agreement introduces significant uncertainty. It creates an in-text contradiction because it provides no specific information on which jurisdictions the agreement is being enforced within, and provides no jurisdiction for dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to the laws of conflict of laws. The Receiving Party and its Representatives agree to adhere to mutually agreeable dispute resolution methods.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies jurisdiction (New York), venue, and service of process. The changed text replaces these specifics with 'mutually agreeable dispute resolution methods.' The contradicted text also mentions adherence to 'mutually agreeable dispute resolution methods' while stating New York law governs the agreement. By removing the specific jurisdiction details and replacing it with a general statement about mutually agreeable methods, it contradicts the clause establishing New York as the governing law because it removes the procedure on how New York jurisdiction would be enforced. This is a direct conflict, making enforcement unclear, so it's a YES.
